full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Low-Profile V-Block Clamp Jaw'. The broader use
case is: Used on a milling machine vise to hold small round stock or tubing without raising the
workpiece too high above the jaws. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by:
Hardened rectangular vise jaw insert with machined V-groove, counterbored mounting holes, and edge
chamfers. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part
must perform this mechanical role: Provides a centered V-groove contact surface and clamp relief
features so cylindrical work can be gripped repeatably. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Combines prismatic jaw geometry with angled groove faces, hole placement,
symmetry, relief cuts, and chamfered working edges. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel,
normalized using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw
oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and
edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 80 mm x 65 mm x 55 mm.
functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body

functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 7 mm on the length centerline at X=13 mm and X=67 mm, Y=32 mm.
functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern

functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 30 mm long x 17 mm wide through the part, centered at X=40 mm, Y=32 mm.
functional_features.name: functional center feature

functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 25 mm x 21 mm x 24 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.
functional_features.name: top relief pocket

functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.
functional_features.name: edge chamfers

functional_features.description: Cut a 90 degree V-groove along the full X length on the top face, groove mouth 32 mm wide and depth 18 mm.
functional_features.name: centered V-groove

functional_features.description: Machine one top reference face at 18 degrees over the last 18 mm of length.
functional_features.name: machined angled reference face

functional_features.description: Add a central counterbore diameter 25 mm x 6 mm deep around the center feature.
functional_features.name: counterbored seat

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Adjustable Drill Press Depth Stop Collar'. The
broader use case is: Accessory fitted around a drill press quill or stop rod for quickly setting
repeatable drilling depth in a small workshop. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied
by: Split cylindrical collar with clamp screw boss, radial slot, bored center hole, and knurled or
faceted grip rim. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled.
The part must perform this mechanical role: Locks at a chosen height and provides a positive
mechanical stop for repeated hole depths. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Requires accurate cylindrical modeling, split clamp features, screw clearance, boss
integration, and small ergonomic surface details. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel,
normalized using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside,
face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No
separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 86 mm and length 108 mm from solid round bar.
functional_features.name: single cylindrical body

functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 28 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.
functional_features.name: axial through bore

functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 38 mm x 3 mm deep.
functional_features.name: end counterbores

functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 28 mm wide over 100 mm of length, centered on the top side.
functional_features.name: milled reference flat

functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M6 holes through the top flat at X=36 mm and X=72 mm.
functional_features.name: radial clamp holes

functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 3 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.
functional_features.name: split relief slit

functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.
functional_features.name: outside edge treatment

functional_features.description: Machine one transverse slot 5 mm wide across the top flat at X=54 mm, depth 8 mm.
functional_features.name: cross relief slot

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'T-Slot Step Clamp Toe'. The broader use case is: A
replacement toe piece for a milling table step-clamp set used to press down on irregular workpieces.
The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined clamp toe with serrated step
profile, radiused contact nose, elongated bolt slot, and hardened bearing pad. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Transfers clamping force from a strap clamp onto the workpiece while allowing height
adjustment from the stepped rear surface. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Tests stepped geometry, elongated slots, angled contact surfaces, serrations, fillets, and
load-bearing proportions. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using
rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all
datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into
that same solid piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Lathe Carriage Dial Indicator Holder'. The broader
use case is: Mounts a dial indicator to a small engine lathe carriage for measuring travel or
setting repeatable stops. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Compact machined
arm with dovetail or clamp mount, indicator stem bore, pinch slot, and screw boss. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Positions an indicator stem rigidly near the work axis while allowing fine orientation
adjustment. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Involves intersecting
bores, clamp slots, compact offset arms, functional clearances, and orientation-critical reference
faces. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using round bar stock. Start from
one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill
secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the
deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Quick-Change Toolpost Boring Bar Sleeve'. The
broader use case is: Insert sleeve used in a lathe quick-change tool holder to adapt smaller boring
bars to a larger holder bore. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Precision
cylindrical sleeve with through bore, external flat, split relief slit, and set-screw dimples. All
other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform
this mechanical role: Centers and supports a boring bar while giving set screws flat bearing
surfaces and preventing rotation. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Tests concentric cylindrical features, thin-wall part representation, flats on round stock, slots,
and small localized contact pockets. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized
using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both
ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate
inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Machinist Parallel Keeper Clip'. The broader use
case is: Small shop accessory used to keep matched parallels paired and upright in a vise drawer or
setup tray. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Spring-style U-shaped machined
clip with relief grooves, finger tab, and soft-contact inner pads or pockets. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Holds two parallels together at their ends without damaging precision ground faces. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires thin-section geometry, internal
clearances, rounded transitions, contact pockets, and nontrivial open U-shaped topology. The part is
made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet
or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features
into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate
welded pieces.

Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Bench Grinder Tool Rest Angle Block'. The broader
use case is: Fixture used on a bench grinder tool rest to quickly set a repeatable sharpening angle

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Bicycle disc brake flat-mount adapter with cooling
fins'. The broader use case is: Road or gravel bicycle adapting a caliper to a larger rotor size The
chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined brake adapter with stepped mounting
pads, through holes, counterbores, rotor-clearance arch, and integral fins All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Offsets the brake caliper to the correct rotor radius while adding heat-dissipating surface
area It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires precise hole spacing,
parallel datum surfaces, curved clearance envelopes, thin repeated fin geometry, and functional
offsets The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block
stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then
machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid
piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Scooter rear suspension rocker link'. The broader
use case is: Light electric scooter with compact rear swingarm suspension The chosen deliverable is

to a small shock absorber with defined leverage ratio It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Tests multi-axis pivot relationships, organic but machinable link profiles,
boss thickness transitions, pocketing, and bearing-seat precision The part is made from low-carbon
steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block
or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces,
grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Folding bicycle handlebar latch cam'. The broader
use case is: Folding bicycle handlepost latch mechanism The chosen deliverable is only the metal
body implied by: Machined eccentric cam lever with pivot bore, cam surface, finger relief, and
latch-pin contact pad All other product elements are external reference items and must not be
modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Applies over-center clamping force to secure
the folding handlebar post It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires
eccentric surfaces, smooth cam profiles, ergonomic lever shaping, contact patches, and clear
representation of over-center locking geometry The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or
equivalent using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside,
face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No
separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'E-bike mid-drive motor torque arm spline clamp'.
The broader use case is: Retrofitted or modular e-bike mid-drive system mounted near the bottom
bracket The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined split clamp arm with
spline-like bore features, pinch bolt ears, offset arm, and frame contact pad All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Resists motor reaction torque by clamping around a splined or keyed interface and tying into
the frame It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests internal keyed
geometry, split-clamp behavior, bolt ear symmetry, torque-load path clarity, and blended transitions
from circular clamp to structural arm The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized
using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both
ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate
inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Bicycle dynamo hub wire-exit strain relief cap'.
The broader use case is: Touring bicycle front hub with integrated dynamo wiring The chosen
deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined protective cap with hub-interface bore, wire
channel, clamp screw boss, curved outer shield, and anti-rotation tab All other product elements are
external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role:
Protects the electrical connector and guides the wire away from the spinning hub and spokes It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests small protective shell geometry,
internal channels, anti-rotation features, screw bosses, and spatial clearance around rotating
bicycle components The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or
plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes,
slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are
bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Conical Burr Coffee Grinder Adjustment Collar'. The
broader use case is: Manual or electric burr coffee grinder used for espresso-to-French-press grind
adjustment. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Threaded stepped adjustment
collar with detent pockets and burr mounting lands. All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Holds and
indexes the upper conical burr while allowing fine axial position changes. It is intentionally
included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines internal threads, concentric stepped bores,
radial detent features, tight coaxial references, and small repeated pockets around a circular body.
The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using round bar stock. Start from one cut
length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary
flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the
deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Meat Grinder Feed Auger'. The broader use case is:
Countertop meat grinder or mixer attachment for sausage and ground meat preparation. The chosen
deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Tapered helical auger screw with drive coupling and
front knife pilot. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled.
The part must perform this mechanical role: Draws meat from the hopper and compresses it toward the
cutting knife and extrusion plate. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Requires continuous helical flights, changing pitch or diameter, blended root fillets, keyed drive
geometry, and a functional transition from screw body to shaft. The part is made from 1045 medium-
carbon steel, normalized using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or
plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces,
grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

or equivalent using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside,
face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No
separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

broader use case is: Reusable mechanical handle for dental impression trays that allows controlled
angular positioning before impression material is loaded. The chosen deliverable is only the metal
body implied by: Machined ratcheting handle yoke with curved tooth sector, cross-pin holes, thumb-
release pocket, and tray dovetail receiver. All other product elements are external reference items
and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Locks the tray at selectable
angles for easier placement in different patient mouth geometries. It is intentionally included in
the SubCAD limit corpus because: Includes curved ratchet teeth, mating dovetail geometry, yoke arms
with aligned bores, ergonomic recesses, and load-bearing fillets. The part is made from AISI 316
stainless steel using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the
outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as
needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

use case is: Fixture used during pipette calibration to hold small vessels or weighing boats in a

point feet, and tweezer access cutouts. All other product elements are external reference items and

consistently while minimizing contact area and trapped liquid. It is intentionally included in the
SubCAD limit corpus because: Challenges representation of shallow curved seating surfaces, radial

rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets,

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Collaborative Robot Vacuum Gripper Manifold Block'.
The broader use case is: A compact pneumatic manifold mounted at the wrist of a collaborative robot
for picking flat packaging, sheet metal blanks, or molded plastic panels. The chosen deliverable is
only the metal body implied by: CNC-machined aluminum manifold body with internal cross-drilled
passages, threaded vacuum ports, O-ring face seals, and robot flange mounting holes. All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Distributes vacuum from one inlet to multiple suction cup ports while providing
mounting features for the robot wrist and cup stems. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Requires representing intersecting internal channels, sealed port bosses,
counterbored mounting patterns, face grooves, and asymmetric port layout in one compact part. The
part is made from AISI 316 stainless steel using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or
plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into
that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded
pieces.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Servo Gripper Finger Quick-Change Coupler'. The
broader use case is: A toolroom adapter used on an electric parallel gripper so operators can swap
custom fingers between product changeovers without removing the gripper body. The chosen deliverable
is only the metal body implied by: Machined steel coupler jaw with dovetail slide, spring plunger
pocket, dowel holes, clamp screw bore, and finger attachment interface. All other product elements
are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role:
Locks a removable gripper finger insert into a repeatable datum position using a wedge clamp and
locating pins. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines sliding
dovetail geometry, precision locating features, clamping clearances, threaded access holes, and
nontrivial datum surfaces. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using
rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all
datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into
that same solid piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Robot Wrist Compliance Cartridge Housing'. The
broader use case is: A small passive compliance module fitted between a robot wrist and a peg-in-
hole insertion tool. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Cylindrical machined
housing with nested bearing seats, radial spring pockets, retaining ring grooves, and bolt-circle
interfaces on both ends. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be
modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Allows limited radial and angular float while
constraining axial motion during automated assembly. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Tests concentric stepped bores, internal grooves, circular patterns, spring
pocket arrays, tolerance-sensitive bearing seats, and multi-interface alignment. The part is made
from 6061-T6 aluminum using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the
outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as
needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Vision Camera Kinematic End-Effector Mount'. The
broader use case is: A rigid adjustable mount for placing an inspection camera beside a robot-
mounted dispensing or picking tool. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by:
Machined aluminum angled saddle with slotted camera rail, dowel-located robot-side pad, cable relief
channel, and clamping screw bosses. All other product elements are external reference items and must
not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Positions a camera at a repeatable
angled offset from the tool centerline while allowing fine setup adjustment. It is intentionally
included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires angled reference planes, elongated slots,
intersecting cable relief cuts, offset mounting datums, and a mix of aesthetic and functional
chamfers. The part is made from 6061-T6 aluminum using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of
round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats,
slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Delta Robot Pick Head Rotary Nozzle Adapter'. The
broader use case is: A lightweight adapter used on a delta robot pick head to mount a small rotary
vacuum nozzle for orienting lightweight parts in flight. The chosen deliverable is only the metal
body implied by: Turned and milled aluminum adapter with central through-bore, keyed shaft pocket,
nozzle thread, wrench flats, and radial set-screw holes. All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Connects the
rotary actuator shaft to a vacuum nozzle while routing vacuum through the rotating center. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests revolved shaft geometry combined
with milled flats, keyed internal pockets, coaxial flow passages, cross holes, and small fastener
details. The part is made from 6061-T6 aluminum using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of
round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats,
slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

broader use case is: A machined nosepiece for a robot-mounted screwdriving spindle used on appliance

and stepped depth references. The part is made from 6061-T6 aluminum using round bar stock. Start
from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill
secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the
deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Pneumatic Sprue Cutter Robot Adapter Clevis'. The
broader use case is: A custom adapter that mounts a pneumatic sprue cutter to a robot wrist for
trimming molded plastic parts after ejection. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied
by: Machined steel clevis bracket with angled wrist flange, forked cutter pocket, cross-pin holes,
gusseted ribs, and clearance scallops around the blade path. All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Holds the
cutter at a fixed offset angle while resisting cutting loads and allowing blade clearance near the
part. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests fork geometry, angled
mounting faces, load-bearing ribs, coaxial pin holes across separated ears, and swept clearance
cutouts tied to tool motion. The part is made from 6061-T6 aluminum using round bar stock. Start
from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill
secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the
deliverable.

functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 33 mm and length 117 mm from solid round bar.
functional_features.name: single cylindrical body

functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 8 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.
functional_features.name: axial through bore

functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 18 mm x 3 mm deep.
functional_features.name: end counterbores

functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 11 mm wide over 109 mm of length, centered on the top side.
functional_features.name: milled reference flat

functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M8 holes through the top flat at X=39 mm and X=78 mm.
functional_features.name: radial clamp holes

functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 3 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.
functional_features.name: split relief slit

functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.
functional_features.name: outside edge treatment

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Conveyor Diverter Mini Rocker Arm'. The broader use
case is: A compact lever inside a small automation cell that flips a paddle to divert rejected parts
from a conveyor lane. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined aluminum
rocker arm with pivot bearing bore, rod-end clevis hole, offset paddle boss, hard-stop pads, and
lightening pockets. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled.
The part must perform this mechanical role: Transfers motion from a pneumatic cylinder rod to a
pivoting diverter paddle with defined mechanical stops. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Combines multiple functional axes, bearing bores, offset bosses, load paths,
stop surfaces, and pockets that are not simple rectangular cuts. The part is made from low-carbon
steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block
or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces,
grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Force-Torque Sensor Cable-Safe Spacer Ring'. The
broader use case is: A spacer mounted between a robot wrist and force-torque sensor where sensor
cables must exit without being crushed or over-bent. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body
implied by: Circular machined spacer ring with dual bolt circles, counterbores, central clearance
bore, radial cable windows, and relieved cable bend channels. All other product elements are
external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role:
Provides a precise standoff and bolt pattern conversion while protecting radial cable exit paths. It
is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests annular geometry, two clocked
bolt patterns, radial openings through a ring, counterbore depth control, and cable-clearance
sculpting. The part is made from 6061-T6 aluminum using rectangular block stock. Start from one
rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets,
holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Micro Gripper Parallel Jaw Stroke Limiter'. The
broader use case is: An accessory mounted onto a miniature pneumatic gripper used for handling
fragile medical or electronic components. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by:
Machined stainless stop bridge with two precision stop screws, gripper body mounting holes, jaw
clearance slots, and replaceable contact pads. All other product elements are external reference
items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Limits jaw travel and
adds adjustable hard stops so delicate parts are not over-compressed. It is intentionally included
in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires narrow clearance slots, symmetric but adjustable stop
features, threaded insert pockets, compact fastener access, and tight packaging around moving jaws.
The part is made from AISI 316 stainless steel using rectangular block stock. Start from one
rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets,
holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Brake Caliper Piston Wind-Back Adapter'. The
broader use case is: Automotive brake service tool used when retracting rear brake caliper pistons
with integrated parking brake mechanisms. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by:
Cylindrical adapter puck with opposing drive lugs, central square-drive socket, chamfered rim, and
relief grooves. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The
part must perform this mechanical role: Engages the piston face slots while transmitting axial
pressure and rotation from a service wrench or press tool. It is intentionally included in the
SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires precise coaxial features, raised lugs on a circular face,
socket geometry, chamfers, and functional clearances between shallow and deep features. The part is
made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using round bar stock. Start from one cut length
of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats,
slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Oxygen Sensor Offset Wrench Head'. The broader use
case is: Exhaust repair tool for removing oxygen sensors in tight engine bays without disconnecting
the wiring harness first. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Offset crowfoot-
style wrench head with hex bore, open cable slot, square-drive boss, and reinforced curved neck. All
other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform
this mechanical role: Grips the hex body of an oxygen sensor while leaving a side slot for the
sensor wire to pass through. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Combines interrupted hex geometry, asymmetric openings, load-bearing transitions, bosses, and
nontrivial wall thickness constraints. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or
equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw
oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and
edge treatments into that same solid piece.

Make the single metal part for the product idea 'CV Boot Clamp Crimping Anvil'. The broader use case
is: Drivetrain service fixture used to crimp ear-style CV boot clamps consistently during axle
repair. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Small hardened anvil block with
contoured saddle, clamp-ear pocket, jaw guide channels, and mounting holes. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Supports the clamp ear and guides plier jaws so the crimp closes to a repeatable height
without damaging the boot. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests
concave saddles, small radiused pockets, parallel guide features, compact hole placement, and
service-clearance-driven geometry. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using
round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends,
bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts
or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Wheel Stud Press Support Cup'. The broader use case
is: Hub repair hardware used with a shop press or C-clamp tool to remove and install wheel studs
without bending the hub flange. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Thick
annular cup with stepped bore, relieved side window, flange contact face, and anti-slip outer flats.
All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must
perform this mechanical role: Supports the hub around the stud head while leaving clearance for the
stud to pass through during pressing. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Includes nested cylindrical bores, stepped internal cavities, side cutouts, thick-section
proportions, and datum-critical bearing surfaces. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel,
normalized using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside
profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are
called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Tie Rod End Taper Inspection Gauge'. The broader
use case is: Steering inspection aid for checking whether a tie rod end taper or steering knuckle
bore is worn beyond acceptable fit. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by:
Machined tapered plug gauge with shoulder stop, engraved depth bands, knurled grip section, and flat
reference face. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The
part must perform this mechanical role: Provides reference taper surfaces and depth marks to compare
seating depth and contact pattern. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Tests tapered solids, shoulder transitions, engraved or recessed markings, mixed grip textures, and
tolerance-sensitive reference surfaces. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or
equivalent using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside,
face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No
separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats,
slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Brake Rotor Runout Indicator Clamp'. The broader
use case is: Brake inspection aid for mounting a dial indicator to a suspension upright or caliper
bracket when measuring rotor runout. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Split
clamp block with V-groove jaw, cross-bolt slot, indicator stem bore, and perpendicular locking screw
boss. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must
perform this mechanical role: Clamps to an irregular service location and provides a rigid
adjustable mount for an indicator stem. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Challenges representation of split bodies, V-grooves, intersecting bores, clamp slots,
bosses, and perpendicular fastening features. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel,
normalized using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw
oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and
edge treatments into that same solid piece.

is: Engine service jig for extracting stuck fuel injectors from a cylinder head without prying
against fragile plastic or aluminum surfaces. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied

and elongated mounting slots. All other product elements are external reference items and must not
be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Spans across nearby head features and guides
a pull screw aligned with the injector axis. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Combines arched relief geometry, slots, threaded boss placement, planar support feet, and
alignment-critical central features. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent
using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face
all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments
into that same solid piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Door Hinge Pin Alignment Jig'. The broader use case
is: Body repair jig used when replacing worn door hinge pins and bushings on vehicles with heavy
doors. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Clamp-on alignment block with dual
hinge-barrel saddles, guide bushing bore, tightening screw pads, and datum shoulders. All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Holds the hinge leaves coaxial while guiding a replacement pin or reamer through
both hinge barrels. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires
coaxial guide features, paired saddles, clamp pads, shoulders, and constrained access geometry tied
to a real repair workflow. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using
round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends,
bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts
or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 60 mm and length 53 mm from solid round bar.
functional_features.name: single cylindrical body

functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 12 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.
functional_features.name: axial through bore

functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 22 mm x 3 mm deep.
functional_features.name: end counterbores

functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 20 mm wide over 45 mm of length, centered on the top side.
functional_features.name: milled reference flat

functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M5 holes through the top flat at X=17 mm and X=35 mm.
functional_features.name: radial clamp holes

functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 3 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.
functional_features.name: split relief slit

functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.
functional_features.name: outside edge treatment

Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.

Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'Door Hinge Pin Alignment Jig'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.
part_name: Door Hinge Pin Alignment Jig - single metal part

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Fanless Mini PC Finned Top Shell'. The broader use
case is: A compact living-room or office mini PC that uses its upper enclosure as the primary
passive heat sink. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: CNC-machined aluminum
top enclosure with integrated fin array, internal boss pads, gasket groove, and corner fastener
wells. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part
must perform this mechanical role: Protect internal electronics while conducting heat from the
processor module to exterior cooling fins. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Combines thin enclosure walls, dense repeated fins, internal mounting geometry, thermal
contact pads, edge radii, recessed screw pockets, and sealing features in one part. The part is made
from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one
rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets,
holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body

functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 11 mm on the length centerline at X=11 mm and X=79 mm, Y=27 mm.
functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern

functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 30 mm long x 9 mm wide through the part, centered at X=45 mm, Y=27 mm.
functional_features.name: functional center feature

functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 25 mm x 18 mm x 5 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.
functional_features.name: top relief pocket

functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.
functional_features.name: edge chamfers

functional_features.description: Tap one side hole M4 from the right long edge into the central pocket; hole axis is parallel to Y.
functional_features.name: side tapped hole

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Foldable Laptop Torque Hinge Barrel'. The broader
use case is: A premium ultrabook hinge assembly connecting the display lid to the keyboard deck. The
chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined hinge knuckle barrel with alternating
hinge ears, axial bore, cable relief slot, torque washer seats, and anti-rotation flats. All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Provide controlled rotational resistance while packaging a display cable channel
through the hinge region. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires
coaxial cylindrical features, segmented hinge leaves, tight clearances, slots through curved
geometry, nested bores, and asymmetric mechanical stops. The part is made from low-carbon steel,
ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or
plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces,
grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Adjustable Tablet Drawing Stand Spine'. The broader
use case is: A desktop stand for a large drawing tablet used by illustrators and industrial
designers. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined rear support spine
with ratchet arc, pivot bosses, cable pass-through, rubber foot pockets, and weight-reducing side
pockets. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part
must perform this mechanical role: Support the tablet at multiple locked drawing angles while
routing charging cable clearance behind the device. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit
corpus because: Tests curved ratchet teeth, angled support surfaces, paired pivots, pocketed
lightweight structure, cable routing geometry, and repeated locking detents. The part is made from
4140 alloy steel, prehard using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn
the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as
needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Smartphone Repair Alignment Clamp Jaw'. The broader
use case is: A service bench fixture for holding smartphones during screen separation, adhesive
curing, or frame straightening. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined
precision clamp jaw with stepped contact pads, reliefs for side buttons, camera bump clearance,
dovetail slide interface, and replaceable soft insert pockets. All other product elements are
external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Secure
a phone frame without crushing buttons, camera bumps, antennas, or fragile glass edges. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Introduces product-specific reliefs,
asymmetric stepped surfaces, sliding fixture geometry, shallow pockets, tight edge references, and
small service-tool features. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using
rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all
datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into
that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: dovetail slide form

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Gaming Console SSD Expansion Heat Sink Cover'. The
broader use case is: An aftermarket internal cover for a console storage expansion bay that cools a
high-speed NVMe SSD. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Low-profile machined
aluminum bay cover with underside thermal pad land, top-side ribbing, latch notch, screw tab, and
airflow chamfers. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled.
The part must perform this mechanical role: Replace the stock bay cover while spreading SSD heat to
exposed ribs and maintaining console airflow clearance. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Tests a shallow constrained envelope, mixed top and underside detail, ribs,
thin walls, offset fastening features, latch geometry, and thermal interface surfaces. The part is
made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat
sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief
features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not
separate welded pieces.

steel, prehard using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the
outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as
needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Camera Monitor Swivel Mount Yoke'. The broader use
case is: A compact field monitor mount for mirrorless camera rigs used by video creators. The chosen
deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined U-shaped swivel yoke with side trunnion
bores, cold-shoe foot, threaded accessory holes, cable clearance scallop, and serrated friction
faces. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part
must perform this mechanical role: Hold an external monitor with adjustable tilt while resisting
loosening during handheld shooting. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Combines yoke geometry, coaxial pivot bores, serrated circular faces, threaded hole patterns,
undercut-like cable clearance, and camera accessory mounting standards. The part is made from
6061-T6 aluminum using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw
oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and
edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 135 mm x 90 mm x 53 mm.
functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body

functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 12 mm on the length centerline at X=18 mm and X=117 mm, Y=45 mm.
functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern

functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 45 mm long x 16 mm wide through the part, centered at X=67 mm, Y=45 mm.
functional_features.name: functional center feature

functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 33 mm x 30 mm x 18 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.
functional_features.name: top relief pocket

functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.
functional_features.name: edge chamfers

functional_features.description: Cut 11 equal triangular serrations across the rear edge, each 2 mm deep.
functional_features.name: serrated contact edge

functional_features.description: Tap one side hole M5 from the right long edge into the central pocket; hole axis is parallel to Y.
functional_features.name: side tapped hole

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'VR Headset Counterweight Battery Cradle'. The
broader use case is: A rear strap accessory for a VR headset that holds a removable battery pack as
a counterweight. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined curved cradle
with strap slots, battery retention lips, spring latch pocket, ventilation channels, and cable
strain-relief hook. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled.
The part must perform this mechanical role: Secure the battery, spread load across the head strap,
and provide cooling gaps around the pack. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Tests ergonomic curvature, strap-through slots, retaining lips, latch pockets, vent channel
arrays, cable management hooks, and non-rectangular consumer accessory form. The part is made from
low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or
plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into
that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded
pieces.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Docking Station Heat-Spreader Bottom Chassis'. The
broader use case is: A USB-C laptop docking station with multiple high-power ports and internal
regulator heat sources. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined aluminum
lower chassis with internal component islands, thermal pad mesas, connector wall cutouts, screw
bosses, and perimeter assembly ledge. All other product elements are external reference items and
must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Serve as the structural bottom
chassis while spreading heat from power electronics to the desk-facing metal body. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires enclosure datum structure,
connector-specific openings, multiple internal height levels, bosses, perimeter ledges, thermal
pads, and manufacturable corner radii. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or
equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw
oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and
edge treatments into that same solid piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'E-Reader Waterproof Port Service Plug Frame'. The
broader use case is: A replacement service part for a waterproof e-reader charging-port area after
repeated port repairs. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined micro
frame with USB-C port window, O-ring groove, snap-tab reliefs, tiny fastener ears, and exterior
contour matching the e-reader edge. All other product elements are external reference items and must
not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Hold the USB-C receptacle region,
compress a seal, and restore exterior shell stiffness around the port. It is intentionally included
in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests small precise port geometry, seal grooves, thin ears, snap
clearances, curved exterior matching surfaces, and repair-focused integration with an existing
consumer enclosure. The part is made from 4140 alloy steel, prehard using round bar stock. Start
from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill
secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the
deliverable.

functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 78 mm and length 112 mm from solid round bar.
functional_features.name: single cylindrical body

functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 26 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.
functional_features.name: axial through bore

functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 36 mm x 3 mm deep.
functional_features.name: end counterbores

functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 26 mm wide over 104 mm of length, centered on the top side.
functional_features.name: milled reference flat

functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M7 holes through the top flat at X=37 mm and X=74 mm.
functional_features.name: radial clamp holes

functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 6 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.
functional_features.name: split relief slit

functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.
functional_features.name: outside edge treatment

functional_features.description: Machine one transverse slot 8 mm wide across the top flat at X=56 mm, depth 7 mm.
functional_features.name: cross relief slot

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Cage-Mounted HDMI Cable Clamp With Captive Wedge'.
The broader use case is: A compact clamp for mirrorless camera cages that secures a full-size or
micro-HDMI cable near the camera body during handheld video work. The chosen deliverable is only the
metal body implied by: Machined aluminum clamp body with stepped cable channel, cage mounting slot,
captive wedge pocket, and thumbscrew bore. All other product elements are external reference items
and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Prevent cable strain and
accidental unplugging while allowing quick cable removal without detaching the camera cage. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines small asymmetric features,
stepped channels, counterbores, sliding wedge geometry, rounded cable reliefs, and tight clearances
around threaded hardware. The part is made from 6061-T6 aluminum using rectangular block stock.
Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine
the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

use case is: A camera-top accessory bridge for mounting two compact wireless microphone receivers

end-stop lips. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The

corpus because: Requires accurate rail profiles, repeated but mirrored mounting features, undercut-

from 6061-T6 aluminum using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or
plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces,
grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Low-Profile NATO Rail Microphone Shock Mount Base'.
The broader use case is: A base adapter that lets a small shotgun microphone shock mount slide onto
a camera cage NATO rail. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined NATO
clamp block with dovetail jaw profile, locking screw boss, microphone mount boss, and relief
pockets. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part
must perform this mechanical role: Provide fast repositioning of a microphone while locking securely

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Boom Pole Quick-Release Recorder Cradle'. The
broader use case is: A clamp-on holder for attaching a small field audio recorder to a boom pole
during documentary sound recording. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Curved
machined cradle with cylindrical pole clamp saddle, recorder backplate, strap slots, and angled
viewing offset. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The
part must perform this mechanical role: Secure the recorder at a readable angle while isolating it
from rotation around the pole. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Mixes cylindrical grip surfaces, angled planar mounting, strap pass-through slots, filleted
transitions, and ergonomic offsets in one compact part. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon
steel, normalized using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the
outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as
needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Field Radio Mast Guyline Tensioner'. The broader
use case is: Portable communications masts for emergency response, overlanding, and remote survey
teams. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined tensioner body with angled
rope channels, eccentric locking cam cavity, tie-off eye, ribbed grip flanges, and load-rated anchor
hole. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must
perform this mechanical role: Adjusts and locks guyline tension while allowing quick release with
gloved hands. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines rope-path
geometry, eccentric cam clearance, high-load eyelet shapes, grip ribs, and angled through-features
that stress representation of functional mechanical intent. The part is made from low-carbon steel,
ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the
outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If
bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Flush-Mount Inductive Proximity Sensor Housing'.
The broader use case is: Rugged sensor body used on automated machine tools to detect carriage or
fixture position in oily environments. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by:
Cylindrical stainless sensor barrel with external metric threads, hex flats, stepped internal bore,
front sensing lip, O-ring groove, and rear connector pocket. All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Holds an
inductive sensing coil, connector insert, sealing features, and wrench flats while presenting a
precise threaded mounting interface. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Combines coaxial turned geometry, external threading, wrench flats intersecting a cylinder,
sealing grooves, thin front lips, and nested internal bores. The part is made from 4140 alloy steel,
prehard using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face
both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No
separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: end counterbores

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Dial Indicator Magnetic Base Swivel Joint'. The
broader use case is: Articulated positioning hardware for holding a dial indicator on inspection
tables or machine frames. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined ball-
and-socket clamp yoke with split lugs, spherical seat, cross bolt bore, serrated friction faces, and
threaded arm mount. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled.
The part must perform this mechanical role: Allows a metrology arm to pivot and lock at a chosen
angle without slipping under indicator load. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Requires spherical contact surfaces, split clamp geometry, coaxial and offset bores, lug
symmetry, serrations, and assembly-critical clearances. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM
A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate.
Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves,
and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Portable Pressure Calibrator Manifold Block'. The
broader use case is: Bench or field calibration fixture for comparing pressure transmitters against
a reference gauge. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Rectangular stainless
manifold block with intersecting drilled passages, multiple threaded ports, valve cavities,
chamfered edges, mounting feet, and engraved port pads. All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Routes
pneumatic or hydraulic pressure between pump input, reference gauge, device under test, bleed valve,
and isolation valve. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests hidden
internal channel logic, orthogonal port placement, pipe-thread bosses, valve counterbores, sealing
faces, and manufacturable drilling paths. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized
using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face
all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments
into that same solid piece.

heated chamber or pipe wall. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Hex-body
adapter with male pipe thread, tapered ferrule cone, through bore, rear compression nut interface,
wrench flats, and lead-in chamfers. All other product elements are external reference items and must
not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Compresses a ferrule around the probe
sheath while sealing into a threaded process port. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit
corpus because: Mixes tapered sealing cones, axial through holes, external process threads, hex-
prism-to-cylinder transitions, small clearances, and chamfered compression features. The part is
made from 6061-T6 aluminum using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or
plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces,
grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Micro-Sprinkler Stake Head Adapter'. The broader
use case is: Low-pressure garden and greenhouse irrigation using small sprinkler heads mounted on
stakes. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Compact adapter body with barb
inlet, stake socket, nozzle thread, and angled flow passage. All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Connects a
vertical support stake, feed tube, and threaded sprinkler nozzle into one adjustable junction. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests intersecting internal passages,
hose barb profiles, molded-looking external ribs, threaded cylindrical features, and angled
connector geometry. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using rectangular
block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides,
then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same
solid piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Seed Drill Metering Roller'. The broader use case
is: Small manual or walk-behind seed drills for sowing vegetable seeds in rows. The chosen
deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Cylindrical metering roller with radial seed cells,
shaft bore, indexing groove, and end retention shoulder. All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Meters seeds by
carrying them in repeated pockets from a hopper into the drop chute. It is intentionally included in
the SubCAD limit corpus because: Needs repeated patterned cavities on a curved surface, precise
angular spacing, pocket edge radii, shaft alignment, and manufacturable undercut-aware details. The
part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using round bar stock. Start from one cut
length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary
flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the
deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Grafting Knife Folding Lock Bar'. The broader use
case is: Folding grafting knives used by nursery workers and orchard growers. The chosen deliverable
is only the metal body implied by: Thin spring lock bar with pivot hole, blade tang contact face,
thumb relief, and formed spring profile. All other product elements are external reference items and
must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Locks the blade open while allowing
controlled release for folding. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Challenges CAD representation with thin spring geometry, precise contact faces, small relief

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Hi-Hat Clutch Lock Collar'. The broader use case
is: Drum kit hi-hat cymbal stand The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Knurled
cylindrical collar with split clamp, cross screw boss, stepped felt seat, and central rod bore All
other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform
this mechanical role: Locks the top cymbal to the pull rod while allowing controlled felt
compression It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests concentric
stepped geometry, split clamp gaps, knurl-like grip surfaces, and small fastener bosses The part is
made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using round bar stock. Start from one cut length
of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats,
slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Nozzle centering cone for multi-head liquid
filler'. The broader use case is: Bottle filling station where fill nozzles must enter bottle mouths
without striking the rim The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined tapered
centering cup with internal cone, nozzle clearance bore, drain groove, and flange mounting holes All
other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform
this mechanical role: Funnels bottle mouths into alignment beneath a descending fill nozzle It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests internal tapered surfaces,
concentric bores, flange features, annular grooves, and sanitary drainage details The part is made
from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet
or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features
into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate
welded pieces.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Label roll core adapter with web tension hub'. The
broader use case is: Label applicator using different label-roll core diameters across production
runs The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Stepped expanding hub adapter with
tapered wedges, radial expansion slots, center bore, and knurled tension collar interface All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Adapts a label roll to the machine spindle while maintaining concentricity and
frictional tension It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines
concentric stepped diameters, radial slots, wedge tapers, collar interfaces, and assembly-aware
clearance features The part is made from AISI 316 stainless steel using round bar stock. Start from
one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill
secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the
deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Flanged Pump Suction Diffuser Body'. The broader
use case is: Inline centrifugal pump installations in HVAC chilled-water or condenser-water loops.
The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Cast or machined diffuser housing with
offset inlet, flanged outlet, internal strainer seat, drain boss, and removable access cover
interface. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part
must perform this mechanical role: Straightens inlet flow, captures debris, and provides a compact
transition between piping and pump suction flange. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit
corpus because: Combines non-coaxial pipe transitions, flange bolt patterns, internal seating
geometry, angled flow passages, bosses, and service-cover features in one part. The part is made
from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet
or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features
into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate
welded pieces.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Grooved Pipe Butterfly Valve Gearbox Mount Yoke'.
The broader use case is: Valve accessory used on grooved-end butterfly valves in building-service
water systems. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined bridge yoke with
stem bore, actuator bolt pattern, curved saddle underside, anti-rotation lugs, and clearance
pockets. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part
must perform this mechanical role: Supports and aligns a manual gearbox or actuator above the valve
stem while transferring torque into the valve shaft. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Requires accurate coaxial shaft features, mixed circular and rectangular
mounting patterns, curved mating surfaces, pockets, and orientation-critical lugs. The part is made
from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one
rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets,
holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Resistance Sled Harness Tow Cleat'. The broader use
case is: Training sled used for sprint resistance and strength conditioning The chosen deliverable
is only the metal body implied by: Machined tow cleat with rounded strap horn, base bolt pattern,
angled web reinforcement, rope groove, and wear-facing radii All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Anchors a tow
strap or harness line to the sled while allowing quick angle changes under high pulling loads It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines organic strap-contact curves,
angled reinforcement webs, bolt-hole patterns, groove surfaces, and thick-to-thin transitions under
realistic load paths The part is made from 4140 alloy steel, prehard using round bar stock. Start
from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill
secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the
deliverable.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Adjustable Solar Rail Mid-Clamp Body'. The broader
use case is: Rooftop photovoltaic panel mounting systems using aluminum rails and framed solar
modules. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: CNC-machined aluminum mid-clamp
block with stepped contact faces, bolt clearance slot, and serrated underside. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Clamp adjacent solar panel frames to a mounting rail while allowing small frame-height
variations. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires asymmetric
steps, functional serrations, bolt-slot geometry, frame-contact surfaces, and clear distinction
between clamping faces and clearance regions. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel,
normalized using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw
oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and
edge treatments into that same solid piece.

Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Battery Module Compression End Plate'. The broader
use case is: Battery storage packs where prismatic cells are mechanically compressed inside a module
frame. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined aluminum end plate with
tie-rod holes, recessed cell contact field, perimeter rail pockets, and edge stiffeners. All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Apply even compression across a cell stack while tying into side rails and tie
rods. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires large flat datum
surfaces, recessed fields, repeated hole patterns, rail pockets, and stiffness-driven geometry. The
part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using sheet or plate stock. Start from one
flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief
features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not
separate welded pieces.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Field Radio Shock-Isolation Corner Frame'. The
broader use case is: Disaster-response crews carrying portable radios in rough terrain or temporary
command posts. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined corner-frame shell
with elastomer insert pockets, exposed control windows, and lanyard anchors. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Protects a handheld or compact base radio from drops while keeping controls and ports
accessible. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires offset frames,
corner bosses, recessed insert cavities, port cutouts, and nonuniform protective wall thickness. The
part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from
one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets,
holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Collapsible Stretcher Hinge Reinforcement Block'.
The broader use case is: Rescue stretchers used in field extraction, industrial sites, and emergency
shelters. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined hinge block with dual
pivot bores, lock-pin channel, stop faces, and weight-reduction pockets. All other product elements
are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role:
Reinforces a folding stretcher hinge while preserving lock alignment and smooth deployment. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests coaxial holes, load-bearing bosses,
mechanical stops, pocketing, and tight alignment constraints. The part is made from low-carbon
steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar.
Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial
holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
